characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Phil Ken Sebben's reckless daughter and Birdgirl. She inherits his company, Sebben & Sebben, after he dies. Beneath the Mask: At first it seems that Judy lives and breathes for her work as her Birdgirl alter-ego, but it becomes clear that she actually has several emotional hang ups, both as herself and in regard to being Birdgirl. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: She's actually really good at the hero thing as Birdgirl, even if she is a bit over the top about it. Cassandra Truth: It takes her a good while for her to believe her father is dead. Distracted by the Sexy: She can't apprehend Kaptain Khaos because he happens to be hot, though she chooses her soon-to-be-fried-alive friends over him.

characters.name: Judy Ken Sebben / Birdgirl
characters.description: Phil Ken Sebben's reckless daughter and Birdgirl. She inherits his company, Sebben & Sebben, after he dies. Beneath the Mask: At first it seems that Judy lives and breathes for her work as her Birdgirl alter-ego, but it becomes clear that she actually has several emotional hang ups, both as herself and in regard to being Birdgirl. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: She's actually really good at the hero thing as Birdgirl, even if she is a bit over the top about it. Cassandra Truth: It takes her a good while for her to believe her father is dead. Distracted by the Sexy: She can't apprehend Kaptain Khaos because he happens to be hot, though she chooses her soon-to-be-fried-alive friends over him.

characters.name: Meredith
characters.description: General manager andBest Friendto Judy. AKA the Mindtaker. A Mind Is a Terrible Thing to Read: "Fli on Your Own Supply" involves her stress-relieving creation to help disgruntled employees think positively from bad thoughts. The system becomes so oversaturated with negativity that she chooses to redirect regulating the thoughts to her own mind to keep the system working, but it slowly breaks her until she has to call Judy for help. Best Friend: Throughout the show, Meredith helps Judy find a way to properly grieve for Phil after she was told he died. Muscles Are Meaningless: Out of the whole team, she doesn't need muscles to hold the whole team from being sucked into a black hole - all she need are two fingers. No Sense of Personal Space: She can force her mind-taking abilities if she wants to, though she only does so when absolutely necessary.

characters.name: Paul
characters.description: The company's masseur from the Syracuse Pauls, AKA The Feels. Ambiguous Gender Identity: In "The Wanky", he's seen wearing genderfluid and genderqueer flag stickers as a part of his sexuality seminar. Camp Gay: Talks and dresses like one. Cargo Ship: In-universe example. One of the Pride Flag stickers he's wearing in "The Wanky" is the objectum flag, representing attraction to inanimate objects. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: Fears not being listened to, which includes not listening to himself.

characters.name: Charlie
characters.description: The head of PR, AKA Strongarm. Bizarre Human Biology: "Rejuvication" is about her undergoing the titular process where her body cocoons itself every 19 years to rejuvenate. It's a very involved process that requires outside assistance to run smoothly. At one point, anautonomous husk of skinis created that she must absorb for the process to continue correctly. She doesn't know why she is this kind of being (since she seems to be not exactly human, if she ever was). Older Than She Looks: Because of "Rejuvication" she's a lot older than she appears, having a long succession of attendants throughout the process. Only Sane Woman: At times. Other times, she suggests putting Gillian in a coma to keep her from working overtime (although given Gillian's stubbornness, Charlie's idea wasn't really insane).Then again, she did a lot of questionable things for the company including cutting down the biggest redwood tree, skinningbaby sealsalive, and murdering people. Sanity Slippage:Has a very sketchy past when she used to do a lot of crimes in the name of the company.

characters.name: Birdcat
characters.description: Some cat Judy decided to adopt, AKA Birdcat. Happily Adopted: Judy randomly adopts it after saving it from a burning building in the first episode. Team Pet: For the Birdteam as a whole.

characters.name: Evie
characters.description: A daughter of someone working at the company who has somehow become friends with the building, AKA The Big Ten. Companion Cube: The building is one to her, though itisalive. Creepy Child: Is perfectly fine with watching the building torture people or Judy screwing up in saving them. And she spies on people through the vents. Gadgeteer Genius: Is very proficient in technology. Heart Is an Awesome Power: Because she can talk to a building, she can talk tomolecules, which becomes indispensable when restructuring people's bodies that were damaged from consuming Baltimo.

characters.name: Dog in Bucket Hat
characters.description: The head of security. Dirty Old Man:"Pilot" has him send a message to Meredith in a messaging app on her smartphone. He later says that he sent the wrong message (or at least, sent the message to the wrong person).Has a very disgusting fetish for lipstick. "Pilot" has him send a message to Meredith in a messaging app on her smartphone. He later says that he sent the wrong message (or at least, sent the message to the wrong person). Has a very disgusting fetish for lipstick. His Name Really Is "Barkeep": Dog in Bucket Hat, who is a dog in a bucket hat.

characters.name: Gillian
characters.description: Sebben & Sebben's executive secretary, passed to Judy/Birdgirl after the latter becomes CEO. Blessed with Suck: She can extend her pinkies seemingly as far as she wants. It's a hereditary ability she calls a blessing, because it makes her the perfect typist for secretary work, but also a curse for the same reason. Body Horror: Drinking too much of her many different energy drinks somehow causes her to grow larger, gain multiple arms, and sprout the faces of the personalities she was trying to embody. Thankfully, she gets better (though she becomes addicted to her energy drink). She also has her extensible pinkies, which are still jointed. Crazy-Prepared: "Thirdgirl" mentions that Gillian is always prepared to handle anything Judy needs her to do. Literal-Minded: She takes everything Judy tells her seriously, as an order. When Judy tells her "Don't move" she chooses not to move from the spot to the point of risking starvation.  In another instance, when Judy tells her "Do what you think is best" when referring to picking a tea flavor, Gillian (after being overcome by the thought of being given that kind of power) drops everything and flies to Japan to get the best sencha tea to bring back to brew the perfect cup of tea for Judy... only to find out that Judy wantedcoffeeafter all.

characters.name: Scot
characters.description: The head of manufacturing. Cruel and Unusual Death: Brian shoves him into Calypsis after getting fed up with his lack of faith in the product.

characters.name: Brian O'Brien
characters.description: The head of human waste. He sometimes assists the Birdteam when needed. Ambiguously Bi: Claims to be straight, but in "Fli on Your Own Supply" he is very interested in the Chippendale dancers. Hidden Depths: In "Shoot from the Foop", he proves to be a competent DJ. My God, What Have I Done?: Feels very disturbed about killing Scot and has hisShareBearprovide him with therapy. Nice Job Fixing It, Villain!: Brian copied the personal files of all the female Sebben & Sebben employees andtold all major news outlets to publish them in case he can't be contacted.The ladies are pissed at Brian for this.

characters.name: Jessica (from Accounting)
characters.description: Jessica (from Accounting). Almighty Janitor: She can controlallthe employees to do her whims because they're all indebted to her niceness, much to Judy's chagrin. She also happened to power the entire building using her under-desk exercycle. On top of all this, she hasn't cashed in any of her paychecks, and doing so,can make the company go bankrupt. The Heart: Although her work output is stated to be poor, she's the nicest worker in the company who gives candy to people, resupplies the medical aid kit, never forgets a birthday, etc. The Informant:It turns out that she's a key component of the company by learning from all the complaints other coworkers may have that they're not willing to tell Judy instead.

characters.name: Phil Ken Sebben
characters.description: Killed Off for Real: As of "Pilot". According to Dog, they were able to find an eyepatch and one of his feet. Parental Neglect: Judy comes to realize in "Pilot" that her Birdgirl persona developed from her need from attention from Phil, who treated anyone less aggressively zany than he was as beneath his notice.

characters.name: EtanDelVay
characters.description: TheCEOofShareBearCorporation. Affably Evil: He means every word he says when he wants to useShareBearsas therapeutic tools, meant nothing by testing the bears on Sebben & Sebben's employees, and was even remorseful forbuying 20% of the company's stock, saying that he intended togive it back to her afterwards.He is not so forgiving when Sebben & Sebben's employees and Judy maim him for circumstances beyond his control. Body Horror: Gets stitched with hisShareBearcreations after getting grievously injured.

characters.name: Kaptain Khaos
characters.description: AnAffably Evilvillain who's out to tarnish Judy's reputation (and kill her). Freudian Excuse: He hates Sebben & Sebben because he accidentally caused his own parents to get fried alive to death with vegetable oil at his favorite restaurant. History Repeats: He himself is fried alive in a hot vegetable oil explosion. Paper-Thin Disguise: His disguise as his villain alter-ego looks easy to tell apart from his real look.
